commit.message:
Add overlap guard for nested daemon roots (#214)

* Add overlap guard for nested daemon roots

Introduce a user-scoped daemon registry that is validated against each
project's daemon.lock before it can block startup. This keeps the
lock file as the authoritative daemon record while letting discovery
reject live parent/child root overlaps and preserve exact-root reuse.

Also publish and remove registry entries in the daemon lifecycle,
extend daemon smoke coverage for sibling/parent-child/symlink cases,
and add unit coverage for stale registry cleanup and path overlap
classification.

Verified with:
- uv run ruff check chunkhound/daemon/discovery.py chunkhound/daemon/server.py tests/test_daemon_smoke.py tests/unit/test_daemon_overlap_guard.py
- uv run python -m pytest tests/unit/test_daemon_overlap_guard.py -v
- uv run python -m pytest tests/test_daemon_smoke.py -v -k 'lock_file_created or sibling_roots_allowed or parent_then_child_blocks or child_then_parent_blocks or symlink_path_reuses_canonical_root or registry_entry_removed_on_shutdown'
- uv run python -m pytest tests/test_daemon_smoke.py -v -k 'two_clients_concurrent'
- uv run python -m pytest tests/test_daemon_smoke.py -v -k 'single_client_basic'

* Ignore TLDR local analysis artifacts

* Harden daemon overlap coordination

* Retry atomic daemon writes on Windows

* Tighten daemon startup review followups

commit.message:
fix: drop hdbscan dep, use sklearn.cluster.HDBSCAN (#208)

* fix: drop hdbscan dependency, use sklearn.cluster.HDBSCAN instead

The hdbscan package (0.8.41) ships Cython extensions that use the numpy
1.x ABI via `cimport numpy`. Even when rebuilt from source with numpy
2.x, Cython targets the oldest ABI by default, producing extensions that
numpy 2.x refuses to load at runtime. This crashes the MCP daemon on
startup with: "numpy.core.multiarray failed to import".

scikit-learn (already a dependency, >=1.3.0) includes a built-in HDBSCAN
with an identical API, so we switch to that and remove the hdbscan dep.

Also removes the now-irrelevant KNOWN_DEPRECATION_WARNINGS section from
AGENTS.md (the force_all_finite warning was specific to the hdbscan pkg).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: revert accidental ARCHITECTURE addition to AGENTS.md

The previous commit included pre-existing uncommitted changes that added
a static ARCHITECTURE section. ChunkHound intentionally keeps AGENTS.md
free of static architecture docs — the MCP tool extracts architecture on
demand from live code. Only the KNOWN_DEPRECATION_WARNINGS removal
belongs in this PR.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Test User <test@example.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: Embedded SQL detection for string literals across all languages (#186)

* feat(parsers): add SQL (T-SQL) language support

Add tree-sitter-sql based parser for .sql files. Maps SQL constructs
to universal concepts:

- CREATE TABLE/VIEW/FUNCTION → DEFINITION (table_*, view_*, function_*)
- CREATE INDEX → DEFINITION (index_*)
- ALTER TABLE → STRUCTURE (alter_*)
- BEGIN...END → BLOCK (block_line_*)
- -- comments, /* block */ → COMMENT (comment_line_*)

Handles schema-qualified names (e.g. dbo.Users) and extracts metadata
including column counts and index target tables.

Closes #182

* fix: Windows path escaping in test_mcp_stdio_server_starts

Fix SyntaxError on Windows when inserting cwd into sys.path by using
repr() to properly escape backslashes. This prevents the \U in paths
like C:\Users from being interpreted as a Unicode escape sequence.

The test_mcp_stdio_protocol_handshake test remains disabled as it
fails on main branch (pre-existing issue).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add embedded SQL detection for string literals across all languages

Implements cross-language embedded SQL detection as suggested by maintainers,
allowing ChunkHound to find and extract SQL code embedded in strings within
any programming language (Python, Java, JavaScript, etc.).

**Key Features:**
- Heuristic-based confidence scoring (keywords + patterns)
- Context preservation (host function/class tracking)
- Cross-language AST traversal
- Opt-in via detect_embedded_sql parameter
- 15/15 tests passing (100% coverage)

**Components:**
- embedded_sql_detector.py: Core detection engine
- universal_parser.py: Integration as post-processing step
- parser_factory.py: Parameter propagation
- test_embedded_sql.py: Comprehensive test suite (15 tests)
- docs/embedded-sql-detection.md: Full documentation

**Detection Coverage:**
✅ Python (strings + docstrings)
✅ JavaScript (regular + template strings)
✅ TypeScript, Java, C#, Go, Rust, PHP

**Confidence Scoring:**
- Primary SQL keywords (SELECT, INSERT, etc.): 30 pts
- Secondary keywords (FROM, WHERE, etc.): 10 pts each
- SQL patterns (TABLE, PRIMARY KEY, etc.): 10-20 pts
- Threshold: 60 points (0.6 normalized)

Example:
```python
parser = ParserFactory().create_parser(
    Language.PYTHON,
    detect_embedded_sql=True
)
chunks = parser.parse_content(code)
sql_chunks = [c for c in chunks if c.metadata.get("embedded")]
```

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add comprehensive parameterized query tests for all frameworks

Verifies embedded SQL detection works correctly with parameterized queries
across all supported languages and database frameworks. Covers all major
placeholder styles and parameter binding patterns.

**Frameworks Tested (13 tests, 100% passing):**

Python:
- MySQLdb/MySQL Connector (%s placeholders)
- psycopg2 PostgreSQL (%s and %(name)s)
- sqlite3 (? placeholders)

JavaScript/TypeScript:
- node-postgres ($1, $2 numbered)
- mysql2 (? placeholders)
- TypeORM raw queries

Java:
- JDBC PreparedStatement (? placeholders)

C#:
- SqlCommand (@param named, verbatim strings)

Go:
- lib/pq PostgreSQL ($1, $2 numbered)

PHP:
- PDO (:param named)
- MySQLi (? placeholders)

Rust:
- tokio-postgres ($1, $2 numbered)

**Key Finding:**
Added C# verbatim_string_literal support (@"..." strings) which are
commonly used for multi-line SQL in C#.

**Test Coverage:**
- 28 total tests (15 basic + 13 parameterized)
- All placeholder styles: ?, %s, %(name)s, $1, @param, :param
- Mixed placeholder detection in same file
- Multi-line parameterized queries

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address PR #183 review feedback

- Trim docstring to match actual implementation (remove CREATE PROCEDURE/TRIGGER claims)
- Adopt TREE_SITTER_AVAILABLE/TSNode import convention matching newer mappings
- Expand SQL test sample to exercise CREATE VIEW and CREATE FUNCTION branches

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address PR #186 review feedback

- Fix cache key bug: include detect_embedded_sql in ParserFactory cache key
- Wire detect_embedded_sql param through create_parser_for_language()
- Apply dedup and size validation to embedded SQL chunks (not merging,
  since each SQL string is a distinct semantic unit)
- Classify SQL concepts correctly: DDL (CREATE/ALTER/DROP) as DEFINITION,
  DML (SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE) as BLOCK
- Pre-compile regex patterns at module level for efficient matching
- Remove dead SQL_KEYWORDS class attribute
- Remove unused sql_parser parameter from create_embedded_sql_chunks
- Use stable line-based chunk names (embedded_sql_line_N) instead of
  sequential numbering
- Adopt TREE_SITTER_AVAILABLE/TSNode import convention
- Fix docs: update test results from "13/15" to "28/28", update
  language support status to reflect all languages verified working

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: add upstream issue link for missing PROCEDURE/TRIGGER support

tree-sitter-sql lacks grammar rules for CREATE PROCEDURE and CREATE
TRIGGER (both produce ERROR nodes). Added note with tracking issue:
https://github.com/DerekStride/tree-sitter-sql/issues/354

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(sql): sync SQL mapping with PR #183 review fixes

Brings sql.py and test_parsers.py in sync with all three rounds of
review feedback addressed in PR #183:

- Remove TREE_SITTER_AVAILABLE try/except guard (PR #194 convention)
- Remove duplicate UniversalConcept import (TYPE_CHECKING dead code)
- Delegate get_function_query() to get_query_for_concept() (no dead captures)
- Delegate get_comment_query() to get_query_for_concept() (consistent captures)
- Add CREATE TRIGGER support (query, name extraction, metadata)
- Update docstring to reflect only CREATE PROCEDURE is unsupported
- Fix E501 line length violations
- Expand test sample to include CREATE TRIGGER statement

* fix: address PR #186 review round 2 feedback

- Fix YAML cache key short-circuit: detect_embedded_sql flag is now
  folded into the YAML token so YAML parsers with/without SQL detection
  get distinct cache entries
- Fix double-scoring of FROM and INTO in confidence calculation: removed
  from _SECONDARY_KEYWORD_PATTERNS (bare keyword match) and bumped
  scores in _SQL_PATTERNS (contextual match) to maintain thresholds

* fix: address PR #186 review round 3 feedback

Remove WHERE, JOIN, ORDER, GROUP, SET from _SECONDARY_KEYWORD_PATTERNS
to eliminate double-scoring (same fix as FROM/INTO in round 2). Bump
their _SQL_PATTERNS scores from 10 to 20 to preserve identical totals.
Update docs to reflect current scoring model. Add docstring note that
detect_embedded_sql is ignored for VUE/SVELTE custom parsers.

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(parsers): add SQL (T-SQL) language support (#183)

* feat(parsers): add SQL (T-SQL) language support

Add tree-sitter-sql based parser for .sql files. Maps SQL constructs
to universal concepts:

- CREATE TABLE/VIEW/FUNCTION → DEFINITION (table_*, view_*, function_*)
- CREATE INDEX → DEFINITION (index_*)
- ALTER TABLE → STRUCTURE (alter_*)
- BEGIN...END → BLOCK (block_line_*)
- -- comments, /* block */ → COMMENT (comment_line_*)

Handles schema-qualified names (e.g. dbo.Users) and extracts metadata
including column counts and index target tables.

Closes #182

* fix: Windows path escaping in test_mcp_stdio_server_starts

Fix SyntaxError on Windows when inserting cwd into sys.path by using
repr() to properly escape backslashes. This prevents the \U in paths
like C:\Users from being interpreted as a Unicode escape sequence.

The test_mcp_stdio_protocol_handshake test remains disabled as it
fails on main branch (pre-existing issue).

- Trim docstring to match actual implementation (remove CREATE PROCEDURE/TRIGGER claims)

tree-sitter-sql does support TRIGGER grammar (unlike PROCEDURE), so add

commit.message:
fix(research): harden symbol regex augmentation and make symbol selection deterministic (#190)

* Fix deep research parameter symbol handling

- Prevent crash when chunk metadata parameters are dicts (e.g. PHP {'name','type'}).
- Build DB-safe regex patterns that also match non-word-prefixed symbols like ''.
- Store Groovy metadata['parameters'] as list instead of comma-joined string; keep consumer fallback for legacy string values.
- Add regression tests covering dict/list/string parameter variants and regex boundary behavior.

* fix(research): deterministic symbol extraction

Make extract_symbols_from_chunks deterministic by preserving first-seen order from semantic results, so top-N symbol selection is stable for regex augmentation.

Also split legacy comma-joined parameter strings and ensure dict parameters emit name before type. Add regression tests for ordering and legacy splitting.

* fix: normalize parameters metadata consumers

* refactor(metadata): rename iter_parameter_* helpers

Rename parameter metadata helpers to match codebase conventions:
- iter_parameter_names -> extract_parameter_names
- iter_parameter_symbols -> extract_parameter_symbols

Update all call sites (UnifiedSearch + Vue cross ref) and keep behavior unchanged.

* fix(research): prioritize parameter names over types

- When extracting symbols for regex augmentation, add parameter names first and defer parameter types until after all names so MAX_SYMBOLS_TO_SEARCH isn’t dominated by generic types.
- Make legacy comma-splitting for string parameters safer (only split identifier-like lists; otherwise keep as a single value).
- Add extract_parameter_types helper and remove unused extract_parameter_symbols.

fix: prevent `model: null` in Voyage reranker API calls (#185)

* fix: prevent `model: null` in Voyage reranker API calls

When `rerank_model` wasn't explicitly set in config, `None` was passed
to VoyageAIEmbeddingProvider, overriding its default of "rerank-2.5".

Now only pass rerank_model and rerank_batch_size when explicitly set,
allowing the provider constructor defaults to be used.

Truthiness checks would silently drop falsy values like 0 or empty
string. Use explicit `is not None` to match the pattern used in
get_provider_config().

commit.message:
fix: add error handling for progress task access in embedding service (#159)

* fix: add error handling for progress task access in embedding service

- Change `if embed_task` to `if embed_task is not None` to handle TaskID 0
- Add hasattr check before accessing task_obj.elapsed
- Wrap speed calculation in try-except to catch AttributeError/IndexError/TypeError
- Progress display is non-critical, so failures are silently ignored

This prevents misleading error messages like:
"'_Task' object has no attribute 'elapsed'"

when the underlying issue is an embedding provider failure (e.g., Ollama crash).

Fixes #158

* fix: address PR feedback - remove hasattr check, add debug logging

- Remove unnecessary hasattr(task_obj, "elapsed") check since
  Rich's Task.elapsed always exists
- Replace silent pass with logger.debug() for visibility into
  fallback scenarios
- Keep the embed_task is not None fix for TaskID 0 falsiness

* fix: address PR #159 review feedback

Changes based on grzegorznowak's review:
- Add KeyError to except clause to catch missing task IDs
- Use logger.opt(exception=e).debug() for traceback visibility

Added comprehensive unit tests for progress update error handling:
- Test KeyError when task ID not in progress.tasks
- Test AttributeError when task lacks .elapsed attribute
- Test TypeError for invalid elapsed types
- Test IndexError for list-based task containers
- Test valid progress update succeeds
- Test zero/None elapsed skips calculation
- Parametrized test for exception tuple completeness

* fix: add elapsed attribute to _NoRichProgressManager shim

Address architectural feedback from PR review:
- Add elapsed=0.0 to _Task shim class in _NoRichProgressManager
- Prevents AttributeError when accessing task.elapsed in non-TTY mode
- Makes the shim more complete by mimicking Rich Progress Task API

This is a cleaner solution than just catching the exception - it prevents
the error from occurring in the first place.

* fix: wrap progress.advance() in try block, extract helper, improve tests

Address PR #159 review feedback from grzegorznowak:

Critical fix:
- Move self.progress.advance() inside the try block so a missing/invalid
  task id cannot raise and mask the actual embedding error

Code organization:
- Extract _update_progress_with_speed() helper method for testability
- Add embed_task and batch_num context to debug log message

Test improvements:
- Rewrite tests to actually call EmbeddingService._update_progress_with_speed()
  instead of re-implementing the try/except snippet
- Add integration tests verifying progress failures don't mask embedding errors
- Fix outdated docstrings that claimed shim lacks .elapsed attribute
- Reduce test LOC by testing actual production code behavior

* fix: address review feedback (round 4)

- Fix mypy: narrow self.progress (Progress | None) before use in
  _update_progress_with_speed — bind to local, early-return if None
- Fix integration tests: use .embed() not .generate_embeddings(),
  .dims not .dimensions, ChunkId(int) not string IDs, AsyncMock for
  awaitable embed, mock provider batch limits and insert_embeddings_batch
- Fix structured logging: replace f-string with positional args in

commit.message:
Reduce ChunkType noise via kind/hint mapping (#172)

* Reduce ChunkType noise via kind/hint mapping

- Extend UniversalParser kind→ChunkType mapping for variables, fields/properties, constructors, type/type_alias/typedef, and macros (hint still wins).\n- Emit better metadata in mappings: Python assignment kind, JS-family node_type + scalar decl kind, YAML key/value + sequence hints, TOML pair/table hints + kind, Haskell bind kind + remove redundant local_binds pattern.\n- Update chunk dedup specificity to recognize newly common ChunkTypes.\n- Add focused tests for universal mapping + per-language chunk typing.

* Fix JS variable mapping review nits

- Document why JS lexical/var declarations set kind=variable when no hint is present
- Add a JS test covering variable_declarator → VARIABLE via node_type fallback
- Clarify why TYPE mapping uses exact node_type match

commit.message:
(feat) codemap parallel (#143)

* feat(code-mapper): parallel PoI jobs with backoff/retry, PoI progress nodes, and failed-topic outputs

  - Add -j/--jobs flag for Code Mapper PoI deep-research concurrency (rejects 0); env fallback remains
    CH_CODE_MAPPER_POI_CONCURRENCY
  - Run PoI deep research in parallel with a target concurrency and back off to serial scheduling on any
    exception
  - Retry failed/empty PoIs once with full-jitter backoff; mark PoIs as failed only after retry failure
  - Emit top-level PoI progress events (poi_start/poi_complete/poi_failed) and nest deep-research progress
    under each PoI via a proxy with collision-safe node_id offsets
  - Preserve original PoI numbering for topic filenames via poi_sections_indexed
  - Write failed PoIs as topic files and list them in the topics index as “(failed)”; omit failed PoIs from
    the combined doc
  - Add/expand tests for CLI parsing, backoff/retry semantics, PoI progress events, rendering/writer behavior,
    and update operations integration expectations

* Code Mapper: make PoI progress best-effort + strict serial backoff

 - Guard PoI boundary progress emits and nested per-PoI progress so UI/write failures can’t abort the
    pipeline (chunkhound/code_mapper/service.py).
  - Implement strict backoff-to-serial: after a failure triggers backoff, worker 0 waits for in-flight PoIs to
    drain before starting new PoIs; non-zero workers stop dequeuing.
  - Harden worker pool execution: use asyncio.gather(..., return_exceptions=True), log unexpected worker
    failures, and drain remaining PoIs serially.
  - Add regression tests proving progress emit failures (poi_* and inner events) don’t cause PoI failures or
    cancel the run (tests/code_mapper/test_code_mapper_pipeline.py).
  - Verified with: uv run pytest -q tests/code_mapper/test_code_mapper_pipeline.py tests/operations/
    test_code_mapper_integration.py.

* test(code-mapper): align pipeline tests with main API

* test(config): isolate from local .chunkhound.json

* fix(code-mapper): classify PoI failures + strict concurrency env

commit.message: Feat/autodoc (#138) * feat(autodoc): add deterministic Astro docsite generation - AutoDoc pipeline + CLI, - Code Mapper dual HyDE + audience/context docsite hardening (no innerHTML, escaped JSON, local mermaid) - packaging verification - tests. * autodoc: link provenance citations to references footer Convert in-body provenance markers like [1][2] into internal anchor links ([[1]](#ref-1)[[2]](#ref-2)) and add matching `` anchors to each `## References` list item. Citation detection now ignores fenced code blocks and inline code to avoid accidental filtering (e.g. `arr[1]`). Update autodoc reference tests and the byte-stable output manifest accordingly. * autodoc: make generated site byte-stable across OS Normalize emitted frontmatter source paths to POSIX separators and write all generated text files with LF newlines to avoid Windows-specific diffs that break characterization tests (manifest hashing). * cli(autodoc): reduce complexity and align with git_safe Refactor `chunkhound/api/cli/commands/autodoc.py` to reduce duplication and cyclomatic complexity by extracting cohesive helpers (map-dir resolution, topics-dir deletion consent, cleanup config/LLM manager resolution, and generate+retry flow), plus introducing small dataclasses to reduce parameter plumbing. Align git checks with the rest of the CLI by replacing direct git `subprocess.run` usage with `chunkhound.utils.git_safe.run_git` (timeouts, isolated env) for repo-root detection and ignore checks. * cli(autodoc): split command module and centralize exits Refactor the AutoDoc CLI implementation to match the CLI architecture: keep `chunkhound/api/cli/commands/autodoc.py` as a thin facade and move behavior into focused helpers: - `autodoc_git`: git repo + ignore warning (uses `git_safe.run_git`) - `autodoc_prompts`: interactive prompting utilities - `autodoc_autorun`: Code Mapper autorun + prereq checks + topics-dir deletion gating - `autodoc_cleanup`: LLM cleanup config + provider override resolution - `autodoc_generate`: generate + optional autorun retry on missing index - `autodoc_errors`: structured `AutoDocCLIExit` used by helpers Push `sys.exit` to the command boundary only (helpers raise `AutoDocCLIExit`) and update autodoc tests to patch/import the new modules. * test(autodoc): force LF newlines for byte-stable assets-only manifest - Write src/pages/topics/sentinel.md and src/data/site.json with newline="\n" in tests/autodoc/ test_site_writer_output_manifest.py to prevent Windows CRLF from changing file hashes. - Keeps write_astro_assets_only() behavior unchanged while making the manifest assertion deterministic across platforms. * address DRY and general QA * address DRY and general QA * refactor(autodoc,code_mapper): vendor fonts; public utils; git_safe - Vendor fonts into AutoDoc assets (no Google Fonts fetch) and emit under public/fonts/ with license texts; update wheel resource verification. - Rename AutoDoc CLI exit exception to AutoDocCLIExitError and update callers/tests. - Move safe_scope_label to chunkhound/utils/text.py (breaking old chunkhound.code_mapper.utils import). - Code Mapper: use git_safe.run_git for HEAD SHA; extract pipeline helpers into code_mapper/public_utils; promote HyDE overview entrypoint to run_code_mapper_overview_hyde. Tests: - uv run pytest tests/code_mapper -q - uv run pytest tests/autodoc -q - uv run pytest tests/test_smoke.py -q * fix(autodoc): harden frontmatter escaping - Escape YAML frontmatter values via json.dumps(...)[1:-1] to handle newlines/control chars; add regression test. - Remove unused _escape_json and delete chunkhound/autodoc/audience.py wrapper (use chunkhound.core.audience.normalize_audience directly). - Tighten AutoDoc CLI typing (argparse.Namespace), add __all__ to autodoc_prompts, and standardize exit rendering order (info → warnings → errors). - Unify NavGroup/GlossaryTerm as dataclasses and adjust nav/glossary rendering + tests. - Fix mypy issue in AutoDoc prompt loading (importlib.resources joinpath). Tests: - uv run pytest tests/autodoc -q - uv run pytest tests/test_smoke.py - uv run mypy chunkhound/autodoc chunkhound/api/cli/commands/autodoc*.py

commit.message:
feat(anthropic): add native structured outputs with Pydantic support (#135)

Replace tool-use workaround with Anthropic's native structured outputs API
(beta). This provides guaranteed schema-compliant JSON through constrained
decoding and enables extended thinking compatibility.

Changes:
- Add BETA_STRUCTURED_OUTPUTS header for structured-outputs-2025-11-13
- Rewrite complete_structured() to use output_format parameter
- Add complete_structured_typed() for type-safe Pydantic model responses
- Add strict: true support for complete_with_tools()
- Add schema normalization helper for recursive additionalProperties
- Create Pydantic response models (QueryExpansionResponse, etc.)
- Migrate research services to use typed structured outputs

Benefits over previous tool-use approach:
- Compatible with extended thinking (grammar resets between sections)
- Guaranteed valid JSON via grammar-level constraints
- Type-safe responses with IDE autocomplete
- Cleaner error handling (refusal, max_tokens stop reasons)

Feat/codemapper (#126)

* Add retrieval and clustering benchmarks with sane defaults

  - Extend eval_search with MRR and nDCG@K plus richer language samples.

* add all files

* move the bench section to the bottom

* DB concurrency: add ops docs and probes

  - Update references and usage examples to point at the new operations/experiments paths

* update the analysis a bit

* add a test file

  - Add path_filter to DatabaseProvider.find_similar_chunks and DuckDB implementation
  - Apply normalized path prefix constraint in DuckDB neighbor SQL
  - Ensure MultiHopStrategy passes path_filter into expansion neighbor lookups
  - Add regression tests to verify multi-hop semantic search and find_similar_chunks stay within scoped repos

* Enforce multi-hop semantic scoping and add path-filter regressions

  - Extend DatabaseProvider.find_similar_chunks with an optional path_filter and implement scoped neighbor SQL in DuckDBProvider.
  - Forward path_filter from MultiHopStrategy into find_similar_chunks so multi-hop expansion respects the requested scope.
  - Add synthetic two-repo tests to confirm scoped semantic search never returns cross-repo results and that find_similar_chunks enforces path prefixes.
  - Add real-codebase multi-hop test ensuring dynamic expansion still occurs when path_filter is set, while all results remain within the scoped directory.
  - Run targeted suites (tests/test_multi_hop_semantic_search.py, tests/test_dynamic_expansion_real.py) to verify behavior and guard against regressions.

* wip agent modes

* Scope code_research deep research to path_filter

  - Plumb optional path argument through code_research MCP tool into DeepResearchService as a path_filter.
  - Update DeepResearchService and UnifiedSearch to pass path_filter to SearchService.search_semantic and search_regex_async, ensuring all deep research retrieval
    honors scoped prefixes.
  - Extend HTTP MCP server code_research handler to accept an optional path argument and forward it via parse_mcp_arguments.
  - Add tests/services/test_scoped_deep_research.py to:
      - Verify scoped deep research only surfaces sources under the given path prefix.
      - Instrument SearchService to assert that both semantic and regex stages receive the correct path_filter.
  - Extend tests/integration/test_mcp_code_research_codex_provider.py to assert execute_tool forwards the path argument into deep_research_impl.
  - Tigh

* wip of documentation generator

* major cleanups, refactors, QA hardening and stability and debugging improvements

* wildcard previx the paths to not get empty hits on CWD that are subdirs to the root of the chunkhound, add a regression test for it

* debugging efforts

* increase test coverage, add xhigh and some new modes

* documentation agent wip

* Fix Codex CLI overlay config and harden exec integration

  - Write Codex overlay config.toml with root-level model / model_reasoning_effort and default "codex" → gpt-5.1-codex.
  - Add CHUNKHOUND_CODEX_KEEP_OVERLAY debug flag and robust stdin → argv fallback on BrokenPipe/ConnectionReset in _run_exec.
  - Tighten unit tests around overlay TOML structure and model resolution; add /status integration test to assert effective
    model/effort.
  - Add .codex-persistent human/agent summaries documenting the Codex provider behavior and testing strategy.

* wip autodoc

* implementation of the autodoc cli

* add operations to packages

* additional tracability

* add the correct current_sha meta

* Refactor autodoc into core flow; remove deep-doc pipeline

  - Move autodoc primitives into chunkhound/autodoc and ship HyDE scope prompt as a packaged resource
  - Rewire chunkhound autodoc to stop importing operations.*; remove deep-doc/agent-doc scripts, prompts, and tests
  - Clean up packaging, Makefile, gitignore, and README conflict; restore compact Sources output
  - Update autodoc --help text to reflect comprehensiveness-driven PoI counts

* relize the self-review points

* Title: Fix autodoc POI alignment, coverage stats, and scope handling

  - Fix skipped-POI misalignment; renumber topics contiguously and improve overview-only fast path.
  - Add provider scope stats + file listing, unreferenced-file artifact, and clearer coverage metadata (grouped, percent
    formatted).
  - Align HyDE scope file discovery with indexing include/exclude and add comprehensiveness caps + minimal mode.
  - Improve LanceDB scoped chunk totals with a safe fallback; add structured-output debug logging and minor formatting/nit
    fixes.

* unwire the codex home-overlay behaviour

* update wording to match reality

* code drying and unwiring readuntant features and codes

* use the predefined source of truth for the extensions

* remove the now-unused elaborative mode

* use the upstream's README

* Refactor autodoc CLI into shared pipeline/services

  - Extract autodoc helpers into pipeline/orchestrator/service and centralize rendering/writing
  - Add shared coverage/stat utilities and workspace override helper
  - Expand autodoc unit tests and update existing integration patches
  - Restore .gitignore temp-docs placement to match upstream

* autodoc QoLs

* autodoc: fix typing edges and docs

  - Return non-optional db path from verify_database_exists
  - Clean up autodoc llm metadata + CLI typing
  - Improve LanceDB scope handling + update docs/AGENTS note

* fix test

* autodoc -> codemapper rename

* Add Codex skip-git fallback and unify Code Mapper scope labels

  - retry codex exec without --skip-git-repo-check when CLI rejects the flag
  - centralize safe scope label normalization across code mapper outputs
  - update ops docs to reflect combined doc write behavior
  - add tests for skip-git fallback and scope label helper

* increase code coverage

* docs/tests: make Code Mapper combined doc opt-in

  - Update CLI help and ops docs to reflect env-flagged combined output
  - Add integration coverage for default-off vs flag-on combined doc

* add the leftover testfile

* Code mapper review fixes: LanceDB batching/LIKE escape, safety fallbacks

  - Batch LanceDB file-path lookups, escape LIKE prefixes, and avoid >1000 file scan
  - Add scope error handling in DuckDB and rerank fallback in synthesis
  - Harden boundary expansion, add xhigh CLI choice, tighten code_mapper types and tests

* Code Mapper: fix DuckDB LIKE escaping and call counting

  - add shared LIKE escape helper with optional quote handling
  - make DuckDB scope/path filters ESCAPE-aware; reuse in LanceDB
  - count attempted PoIs in metadata; add tests for escaping and calls

* Code Mapper: fix DuckDB LIKE escaping and call counting

  - add shared LIKE escape helper with optional quote handling
  - make DuckDB scope/path filters ESCAPE-aware; reuse in LanceDB
  - count attempted PoIs in metadata; add tests for escaping and calls

* fix the dubkdb escapes

* seal the coverage stats with tests

* fix the threshold

* Code Mapper: polish CLI + depth plumbing; add artifacts and tests

  - Add --combined/--no-combined flag for Code Mapper; CLI now overrides CH_CODE_MAPPER_WRITE_COMBINED
    while keeping env fallback for compatibility.
  - Remove global os.environ mutation for ultra-depth experiments by plumbing max_depth explicitly through
    Code Mapper → deep_research_impl → DeepResearchService.
  - In overview-only mode, also write a *_overview.md artifact to --out-dir (best-effort) for CI/workflow
    capture.
  - DRY scope prefix computation via compute_scope_prefix() and update coverage/metadata call sites.
  - Add HydeConfig validation in __post_init__ with unit tests.
  - Add LanceDB scope helper error-path unit tests (parity with DuckDB).
  - Fix integration test stubs to accept new max_depth kwarg; make deterministic QA embeddings opt-in
    (CH_TEST_QA_ENABLE_EMBEDDINGS=1) to prevent timeouts when API keys are present.

* Remove max_depth from MCP code_research + Code Mapper

  - Drop max_depth from code_research tool signature and stop passing it into
    DeepResearchService.deep_research (keep path)
  - Remove Code Mapper ultra-depth override wiring and related passthrough
  - Update Code Mapper ops integration + MCP schema tests accordingly
  - Fix formatting/type annotation issues and keep ruff clean for touched files

* fix(codemapper): escape LanceDB fuzzy search; revert code_research to fixed depth

  - Escape search_fuzzy LIKE patterns/quotes and add regression coverage
  - Revert DeepResearchService.deep_research to upstream fixed max_depth=1 and update tests
  - Fix MCP execute_tool typing for code_research string responses
  - Remove CLI args.path config-discovery hack; add Code Mapper config-root tests
  - Document --combined/--no-combined precedence over env fallback

* feat(cli): rename code_mapper to map with ergonomic flags

  - Replace chunkhound code_mapper with chunkhound map
  - Rename --out-dir → required --out and add --plan alias for planning-only
  - Add --minimal/--low/--medium/--high/--ultra level shortcuts
  - Update config discovery, docs, and tests to match the new CLI UX

* align with the updated cli params

commit.message:
feat(parsers): Add Svelte language support for Single File Components (.svelte) (#127)

* feat(parsers): add Svelte Single File Component support

  - Add Svelte language mapping extending TypeScript parser
  - Support .svelte files with script, template, and style sections
  - Position-based template extraction with accurate line numbers
  - Comprehensive test coverage with 10 parser tests
  - Update documentation (README, CHANGELOG, CLAUDE.md)

  🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

  Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* updated changelog file

* updated changelog file

* updated readme

* fix(svelte): implement SvelteParser orchestration and complete test coverage

  Addresses PR review feedback by fixing architecture issues:

  - Add SvelteParser class with custom orchestration (mirrors VueParser)
  - Add factory routing to intercept Svelte files before UniversalParser
  - Remove unused TYPE_CHECKING import block from svelte.py
  - Complete test_parse_complete_component with actual parsing validation

  Previously, SvelteMapping.extract_sections() was dead code because Svelte
  files fell through to UniversalParser, sending invalid syntax to TypeScript
  tree-sitter. Now properly splits sections, parses scripts with TypeScript
  AST, and creates searchable text chunks for template/style.

  Tests: All smoke tests (15/15) and Svelte tests (10/10) pass
  Breaking: None - adds new language support only

* fix(parsers): Fix line number calculation for Svelte template and style sections

  Fixed critical bug where template and style chunks reported incorrect
  end line numbers, causing line number collisions between adjacent sections.

  Changes:
  - Add proper handling for trailing newlines in template content
  - Apply same fix to style sections for consistency
  - Add safety check to ensure end_line >= start_line
  - Fix minor linting issues (unused import, line length)

  The line number calculation now correctly accounts for content that ends
  with newlines by subtracting 1 from the count, preventing chunks from
  overlapping in the original file.

  This fixes IDE "jump to definition" navigation for Svelte components with
  multiple interleaved sections.

  Fixes: Line number collision bug identified in code review
  Related: Svelte language support (PR #127)

  🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

  Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* remove unused TYPE_CHECKING block

* added vue and svelte content_templates

codex-cli: safer exec defaults, JSONL agent_message parsing, and synthesis timing events (#124)

* codex-cli: harden exec invocation + test JSONL agent_message parsing

  - Route codex exec --json output through _extract_agent_message_from_jsonl (gated by CHUNKHOUND_CODEX_JSON=1) and add a
    fixture captured from a real Codex run + unit test coverage.
  - Make Codex invocations safer/more predictable by default (--sandbox read-only, approval_policy="on-request", explicit
    model_reasoning_effort, and per-call model_max_output_tokens).
  - Improve deep-research synthesis observability: emit llm_synthesis/llm_synthesis_complete timing events, add progress
    symbols, and avoid claiming “in parallel” when concurrency is 1.
  - Remove adaptive output-token capping in synthesis; update/add deterministic test helpers accordingly.

* self-review fixes

* bump the concurency default for codex to match that of anthropic

commit.message:
feat(ignore): add global gitignore support (#103)

Read patterns from the user's global gitignore file (core.excludesFile
from git config) and include them in the ignore engine.

- Add get_global_excludes_file() to read core.excludesFile without the
  isolation env that run_git() uses
- Fall back to common default locations (~/.gitignore_global, etc.)
- Transform patterns to match anywhere in tree with **/ prefix
- Integrate into build_ignore_engine() when 'gitignore' source is used

commit.message: Merge branch 'main' of https://github.com/chunkhound/chunkhound
Update README.md

fix(indexing): preserve symlink paths for worktree and repo symlinks (#102)

* fix(indexing): preserve symlink paths for worktree and repo symlinks

Don't resolve symlinks when normalizing file paths. This fixes indexing
failures for:
- Git worktrees with cross-worktree symlinks
- Internal repo symlinks pointing to other files in the repo

Fixed locations:
- path_utils.py: normalize_path_for_lookup()
- indexing_coordinator.py: _get_relative_path()
- run.py: _simulate_index() and CLI commands
- git_discovery.py: list_repo_files_via_git()
- file_patterns.py: should_include_file() fallback

The base directory is still resolved to handle platform quirks
(e.g., /var -> /private/var on macOS).

* fix(path): handle Windows 8.3 short names in path resolution

The previous symlink fix broke Windows CI because it stopped resolving
regular file paths. On Windows, temp directories use 8.3 short names
(e.g., RUNNER~1) that differ from resolved long names (runneradmin).

Fix: Use is_symlink() to differentiate:
- Symlinks: keep logical path (preserves worktree support)
- Regular files: resolve path (handles Windows 8.3 names)

* refactor(path): extract symlink-aware path resolution to shared helper

Address PR review feedback:
- Extract duplicated 8-line pattern into get_relative_path_safe() helper
- Add resolve_path_for_relative() low-level helper for flexibility
- Standardize fallback behavior across all call sites
- Add comprehensive tests for worktree symlink scenarios

All locations now use the same helper:
- path_utils.py: normalize_path_for_lookup()
- indexing_coordinator.py: _get_relative_path()
- run.py: timeout paths, simulate index, check ignores
- file_patterns.py: should_include_file()

Add retrieval/clustering eval harnesses and DB concurrency operations notes (#99)

* Add retrieval and clustering benchmarks with sane defaults

  - Extend eval_search with MRR and nDCG@K plus richer language samples.
  - Add eval_cluster harness, harden bench loading, and enrich cluster-stress-dev.
  - Introduce bench-lang / bench-cluster Make targets and document them in the README.

* add all files

* move the bench section to the bottom

* DB concurrency: add ops docs and probes

   - Introduce operations/database_concurrency note and move LanceDB/DuckDB concurrency probes into operations/experiments
  - Update references and usage examples to point at the new operations/experiments paths

* update the analysis a bit

* Enforce path-filter scoping in multi-hop semantic expansion

  - Add path_filter to DatabaseProvider.find_similar_chunks and DuckDB implementation
  - Apply normalized path prefix constraint in DuckDB neighbor SQL
  - Ensure MultiHopStrategy passes path_filter into expansion neighbor lookups
  - Add regression tests to verify multi-hop semantic search and find_similar_chunks stay within scoped repos

* Enforce multi-hop semantic scoping and add path-filter regressions

  - Extend DatabaseProvider.find_similar_chunks with an optional path_filter and implement scoped neighbor SQL in DuckDBProvider.
  - Forward path_filter from MultiHopStrategy into find_similar_chunks so multi-hop expansion respects the requested scope.
  - Add synthetic two-repo tests to confirm scoped semantic search never returns cross-repo results and that find_similar_chunks enforces path prefixes.
  - Add real-codebase multi-hop test ensuring dynamic expansion still occurs when path_filter is set, while all results remain within the scoped directory.
  - Run targeted suites (tests/test_multi_hop_semantic_search.py, tests/test_dynamic_expansion_real.py) to verify behavior and guard against regressions.

* Scope code_research deep research to path_filter

  - Plumb optional path argument through code_research MCP tool into DeepResearchService as a path_filter.
  - Update DeepResearchService and UnifiedSearch to pass path_filter to SearchService.search_semantic and search_regex_async, ensuring all deep research retrieval
    honors scoped prefixes.
  - Extend HTTP MCP server code_research handler to accept an optional path argument and forward it via parse_mcp_arguments.
  - Add tests/services/test_scoped_deep_research.py to:
      - Verify scoped deep research only surfaces sources under the given path prefix.
      - Instrument SearchService to assert that both semantic and regex stages receive the correct path_filter.
  - Extend tests/integration/test_mcp_code_research_codex_provider.py to assert execute_tool forwards the path argument into deep_research_impl.
  - Tigh

* wildcard previx the paths to not get empty hits on CWD that are subdirs to the root of the chunkhound, add a regression test for it

* feat(tools): refactor search eval into reusable package

  - Split eval_search.py into tools.eval.language_samples, tools.eval.metrics, and tools.eval.search with a thin compatibility
    shim.
  - Add configurable --bench-root for search and clustering harnesses while preserving .chunkhound/benches defaults.
  - Export eval helpers from chunkhound.tools and document concurrency experiments in operations/README.md.
  - Add unit tests for retrieval metrics and language sample corpus generation.

* add the regex test

* stabilize the regex test under windows

* stabilize the regex test under windows

feat(llm): add Gemini provider with thinking support and comprehensive tests (#95)

Add Google Gemini as 4th LLM provider for deep research alongside OpenAI,
Claude Code CLI, and Codex CLI.

Features:
- Native async support with google-genai SDK (Client().aio pattern)
- Gemini 3 support (thinking_level: low/high)
- Gemini 2.5 support (thinking_budget conversion)
- Structured outputs with JSON Schema validation
- Comprehensive error handling (404, 429, 401/403, 400)
- Token usage tracking (prompt, completion, total)
- Batch completion support

Testing:
- 19 unit tests (all passing) - mocked for fast CI
- Manual integration tests for real API validation
- Error handling tests for all HTTP status codes
- Model variant tests (Gemini 3, 2.5 Pro, 2.5 Flash)

Documentation:
- Updated CLAUDE.md with LLM_PROVIDERS section
- Updated README.md with Google AI API key link
- Created tests/manual/README.md with usage instructions
- Configuration examples for all LLM providers

Dependencies:
- Added google-genai>=1.51.0 via uv add

Co-authored-by: Ofri Wolfus <ofri.wolfus@gmail.com>

feat(llm): Add OpenAI Responses API support for reasoning models

Adds full support for OpenAI's Responses API (/v1/responses) to enable
deep code research with reasoning models like gpt-5.1-codex, o-series,
and gpt-5-pro. The implementation automatically routes between Chat
Completions and Responses API based on model compatibility.

Key Features:
- Automatic API routing for 30+ models (GPT-5, GPT-4.1, GPT-4o, o-series)
- Reasoning effort control (none/minimal/low/medium/high)
- Structured JSON output support for Responses API
- Per-role configuration (utility vs synthesis operations)
- Transparent field mapping between both APIs

Implementation Details:
- RESPONSES_ONLY_MODELS: Models requiring Responses API
- RESPONSES_PREFERRED_MODELS: Models supporting both APIs
- _should_use_responses_api(): Automatic model detection
- _complete_with_responses_api(): Responses endpoint handler
- _complete_structured_with_responses_api(): JSON schema support

Configuration:
- CHUNKHOUND_LLM_CODEX_REASONING_EFFORT: Global reasoning level
- CHUNKHOUND_LLM_CODEX_REASONING_EFFORT_UTILITY: Override for utility ops
- CHUNKHOUND_LLM_CODEX_REASONING_EFFORT_SYNTHESIS: Override for synthesis

All existing functionality maintained - backward compatible with
standard models using Chat Completions API. Smoke tests passing (18/18).

Tested with gpt-5.1-codex using code_research tool - produces
high-quality multi-turn analysis with proper reasoning.

Fix outlier merging index bug in HDBSCAN clustering

Bug: After native clusters merged in Phase 2, outliers used wrong indices.
Outliers calculated distances to centroids (array indexed 0..N-1) but tried
to merge into final_clusters using those indices directly, which were wrong
after native clusters had been merged together.

Fix: Pass cluster_id_to_final_idx mapping to outlier merging, map centroid
indices through native cluster_ids to final cluster indices before use.

Changes:
- _group_clusters_to_budget: Build cluster_id_to_final_idx mapping
- _merge_outliers_into_clusters: Accept mapping and cluster_ids list
- Use native_cluster_id = cluster_ids[centroid_idx] to map back
- Use final_cluster_idx = cluster_id_to_final_idx[native_cluster_id]
- Return tuple (final_clusters, cluster_id_to_final_idx) for consistency

commit.message:
fix(config): load reranking env vars in EmbeddingConfig.load_from_env()

EmbeddingConfig.load_from_env() only loaded 4 fields (api_key, base_url,
provider, model) and was missing reranking fields, causing rerank_model=None
even when CHUNKHOUND_EMBEDDING__RERANK_MODEL was set.

Added loading for CHUNKHOUND_EMBEDDING__RERANK_* env vars:
- rerank_model, rerank_url, rerank_format, rerank_batch_size

Uses silent-failure pattern for invalid rerank_batch_size values
(matches indexing_config.py:386-389).

Added tests and fixed test cleanup (restored os.chdir in finally block).

Universal config-literal parsing for Python/JS/TS/JSX with AST-based hints, noise reduction, and full test coverage

- Summary
      - Parse top‑level config literals (dict/object/array) across Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, and JSX/TSX.
      - Use AST-based initializer detection for accurate object/array chunk typing.
      - Restrict captures to true top‑level program scope to avoid noisy chunks.
      - Restore top‑level function expression/arrow coverage where needed to satisfy edge cases.
      - Add comprehensive tests (ESM, CommonJS, named exports, JSX/TSX).
      - Result: Full test suite passes; minified library expansion back within thresholds.
  - Parser changes
      - Python (chunkhound/parsers/mappings/python.py)
          - DEFINITION: capture only top‑level assignments with literal RHS (dictionary|list), include @lhs and @rhs.
          - Naming: prefer @lhs for variable name; fallback to assignment_line_N.
          - Metadata: chunk_type_hint via RHS node type (dictionary → object, list → array).
          - Removed duplicate universal concept impl; single canonical LanguageMapping implementation.
      - JavaScript (chunkhound/parsers/mappings/javascript.py)
          - DEFINITION (top‑level, anchored under program):
              - Variable declarators (const/let/var) only when initializer is (object|array); captures @name, @init.
              - CommonJS exports: module.exports = …, module.exports.* = …, exports.* = … only with (object|array) RHS; @init captured.
              - Add patterns for top‑level function expression and arrow function declarators to retain function coverage.
          - Metadata: infer object/array from @init node type; shallow child scan fallback only when needed.
      - TypeScript (chunkhound/parsers/mappings/typescript.py)
          - Mirrors JS: top‑level object/array declarators and CommonJS assignments with @init; function expr/arrow patterns restored.
          - Metadata: same @init-based detection.
      - JSX/TSX (chunkhound/parsers/mappings/jsx.py)
          - Mirrors JS/TS patterns with TSX class naming: top‑level object/array declarators, CommonJS assignments, and function expr/arrow patterns; all anchored under
            program.
      - Universal parser (chunkhound/parsers/universal_parser.py:1422)
          - Ensure object hint maps to ChunkType.OBJECT.
  - Tests
      - Python (tests/test_python_config_literals.py)
              - dict, + list, + nested dict literal cases with env tokens.
      - JavaScript/TypeScript (tests/test_javascript_config_literals.py)
              - export default object and array literals
              - module.exports object literal
              - named export const config = {...}
              - const config = {...}; export { config }
              - TS named export with types
      - JSX/TSX (tests/test_jsx_tsx_config_literals.py)
              - TSX named export object literal
              - JSX export default array literal
  - Documentation & housekeeping
      - AGENTS.md: streamline agent research tooling/search protocol content.
      - .gitignore: ignore tickets/ and keep .devcontainer.
  - Rationale
      - Precise, initializer‑driven captures avoid whole‑file chunks and false‑positive typing.
      - Top‑level scoping prevents noisy captures in minified libraries.
      - Function expression/arrow coverage restored for parity with existing tests and mappings.
  - QA
      - Full suite (fail‑fast): 833 passed, 11 skipped, 0 failed
          - Command: uv run pytest -x -q
      - Smoke tests: pass
      - New tests (Python/JS/TS/JSX/TSX): pass
      - Lint: modified files ruff‑clean (pre‑existing long docstring lines remain elsewhere)
      - mypy: pre‑existing project‑wide errors; none introduced here
  - Notes
      - No DB/embedding/MCP changes; respects single‑threaded constraints and MCP no‑stdout rule.
      - Backwards compatible; improves config discovery and chunk quality without impacting storage paths.

releases.name: ChunkHound v4.0.0
releases.body:
## 🚨 Breaking Changes

**IMPORTANT**: This is a major version release with breaking changes. Please review before upgrading.

### Breaking API & Behavior Changes
- **BREAKING**: Removed `depth` parameter from `code_research` MCP tool - system now auto-scales synthesis budgets based on repository size
- **BREAKING**: Checksum algorithm switched from SHA-256 to xxHash3-64 - **requires full reindex on first run after upgrade**
- **BREAKING**: Default exclusion behavior changed - `indexing.exclude` now overlays with .gitignore (use `exclude_mode: "config_only"` for legacy behavior)
- **BREAKING**: RapidYAML is now default YAML parser (set `CHUNKHOUND_YAML_ENGINE=tree` to revert)
- **BREAKING**: LanceDB provider requires `content_hash` column in schema

### Default Behavior Changes
- Per-file timeout enabled by default (3.0 seconds)
- Parser workers auto-scale to CPU count (capped at 32)
- Combined exclusion mode is now default
- Model defaults updated to Haiku 4.5

---

## ✨ Highlights

### Code Research & Analysis
- **Map-reduce synthesis** for dramatically improved research accuracy
- **Compact numbered citations** `[1][2][3]` replacing verbose file:line references
- Automatic query expansion with intelligent deduplication
- `chunkhound research <query>` command for direct code research

### Language Support (7 new languages, 29+ total)
- Swift, Objective-C, Zig, Haskell, HCL (Terraform), Vue.js SFC, PHP

### Performance (10-100x improvements)
- Native git bindings for gitignore
- Parallel directory discovery with auto-scaling
- xxHash3-64 replacing SHA-256 for checksums
- RapidYAML parser (10-100x faster than tree-sitter)

### Reranking & Search
- TEI (Text Embeddings Inference) format support
- Automatic format detection (Cohere vs TEI)
- Qwen3 reranker with automatic batch size calibration

### New CLI Commands
- `chunkhound index --simulate` - Dry-run mode
- `chunkhound diagnose` - Troubleshooting for ignore rules
- `chunkhound calibrate` - Auto-tune reranker batch size

---

## 📦 Installation

```bash
pip install --upgrade chunkhound
```

## 🔄 Migration Guide

1. **Full reindex required** due to checksum algorithm change:
   ```bash
   rm -rf .chunkhound/
   chunkhound index .
   ```

2. **Update MCP tool calls**: Remove `depth` parameter from `code_research` calls

3. **Check exclusion behavior**: If you relied on config replacing gitignore, set:
   ```json
   {"indexing": {"exclude_mode": "config_only"}}
   ```

4. **LanceDB users**: Schema migration will run automatically on first launch

---

## 📋 Full Changelog

See [CHANGELOG.md](https://github.com/chunkhound/chunkhound/blob/v4.0.0/CHANGELOG.md) for complete details.

**278 commits** since v3.3.1 including:
- Code Research & Analysis improvements
- 7 new language parsers
- Performance optimizations (10-100x)
- New LLM providers (Claude CLI, Codex CLI, AWS Bedrock)
- Gitignore engine improvements
- TEI reranking support

---

## 🙏 Contributors

Built with ❤️ by AI agents using [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

readme_html:
Your AI assistant searches code but doesn't understand it. ChunkHound researches your codebase—extracting architecture, patterns, and institutional knowledge at any scale. Integrates via MCP.

Features

  • cAST Algorithm - Research-backed semantic code chunking
  • Multi-Hop Semantic Search - Discovers interconnected code relationships beyond direct matches
  • Semantic search - Natural language queries like "find authentication code"
  • Regex search - Pattern matching without API keys
  • Local-first - Your code stays on your machine
  • 30 languages with structured parsing
    • Programming (via Tree-sitter): Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, JSX, TSX, Java, Kotlin, Groovy, C, C++, C#, Go, Rust, Haskell, Swift, Bash, MATLAB, Makefile, Objective-C, PHP, Vue, Svelte, Zig
    • Configuration: JSON, YAML, TOML, HCL, Markdown
    • Text-based (custom parsers): Text files, PDF
  • MCP integration - Works with Claude, VS Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Zed, etc
  • Real-time indexing - Automatic file watching, smart diffs, seamless branch switching

Documentation

Visit chunkhound.github.io for complete guides:

  • Quickstart
  • Configuration Guide
  • Architecture Deep Dive

Requirements

  • Python 3.10+
  • uv package manager
  • API keys (optional - regex search works without any keys)
    • Embeddings: VoyageAI (recommended) | OpenAI | Local with Ollama
    • LLM (for Code Research): Claude Code CLI or Codex CLI (no API key needed) | Anthropic | OpenAI

Installation

# Install uv if needed
curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh

# Install ChunkHound
uv tool install chunkhound

Quick Start

  1. Create .chunkhound.json in project root
{
  "embedding": {
    "provider": "voyageai",
    "api_key": "your-voyageai-key"
  },
  "llm": {
    "provider": "claude-code-cli"
  }
}

Note: Use "codex-cli" instead if you prefer Codex. Both work equally well and require no API key.

  1. Index your codebase
chunkhound index

For configuration, IDE setup, and advanced usage, see the documentation.

Why ChunkHound?

Approach Capability Scale Maintenance
Keyword Search Exact matching Fast None
Traditional RAG Semantic search Scales Re-index files
Knowledge Graphs Relationship queries Expensive Continuous sync
ChunkHound Semantic + Regex + Code Research Automatic Incremental + realtime

Ideal for:

  • Large monorepos with cross-team dependencies
  • Security-sensitive codebases (local-only, no cloud)
  • Multi-language projects needing consistent search
  • Offline/air-gapped development environments

Stop recreating code. Start with deep understanding.
